message.content:
Investigate three reported field-behavior bugs in the DesignFlow Angular frontend at C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend. Read-only; report findings with exact file:line citations. Do NOT edit anything.

BUG C: "Factory ID" is rendered as a plain number input field, but it SHOULD be a dropdown/autocomplete linked to the Factories table (user picks a factory by name; the id is stored).
BUG D: "Retail price" does not format the number as a currency/$ amount (it shows a raw number, no $ formatting).
BUG E: "FOB cost" field accepts alphabetic/non-numeric characters when it should only accept numbers.

These appear in the RFQ area — likely the RFQ grid and/or the Add New Product form. Relevant files:
- src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts (column definitions: cellEditor, cellEditorParams, valueFormatter, valueParser for fob_cost, retail price, factory_id)
- src/app/pages/rfq/addNewProduct/addNewProduct.component.* and src/app/pages/rfq/addNewProductGrid/addNewProductGrid.component.ts
- src/app/components/factory/factory.component.* (factory selector — does a dropdown component exist that ISN'T being used for factory_id?)
- search for factory_id / factoryList / getAllVendors or getfactories to see if a factory list is available to populate a dropdown

For EACH of C, D, E: (1) confirm real/not with file:line, (2) root cause (e.g., column uses default text editor with no numeric valueParser; no valueFormatter applying currency; factory_id column uses agTextCellEditor/number input instead of a select/autocomplete editor even though a factory list exists), (3) note whether a correct pattern exists elsewhere in the same file to copy. Quote the key column-definition lines.

375	  private filterCategoriesByDivision(): void {
376	    if (!this.selectedDivisionCode) {
377	      this.mgCategories = [];
378	      return;
379	    }
380	
381	    const divId = this.selectedDivisionCode.divCode_id;
382	
383	    if (divId === POP_LIC_DIV_CODE_ID) {
384	      const allowedPop = ['Wall', 'Tabletop', 'Clock'];
385	      this.mgCategories = this.allMgCategories.filter(cat =>
386	        allowedPop.some(allowed => cat.mgCategory && cat.mgCategory.toLowerCase().includes(allowed.toLowerCase()))
387	      );
388	    } else if (divId === SPRUCE_LIC_DIV_CODE_ID) {
389	      const allowedSpruceLic = ['Storage', 'Workspace', 'Floor', 'Garden'];
390	      this.mgCategories = this.allMgCategories.filter(cat =>
391	        allowedSpruceLic.some(allowed => cat.mgCategory && cat.mgCategory.toLowerCase().includes(allowed.toLowerCase()))
392	      );
393	    } else {
394	      this.mgCategories = [...this.allMgCategories];
395	    }
396	
397	    if (this.selectedCategory && !this.mgCategories.find(c => c.id === this.selectedCategory.id)) {
398	      this.onCategoryChange(null);
399	    }
400	  }

542	      control.setValue(String(value));
543	      return;
544	    }
545	    if (typeof value === 'object') {
546	      const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
547	      if (typeof record.title === 'string') {

549	        return;
550	      }

853	  private _filter(value: any, data: any[]): any[] {
854	    if (!value) {
855	      return data; // If value is null/undefined, return the full dataset (prevents dropdown clearing)
856	    }
857	
858	    let filterValue: string;
859	
860	    if (typeof value === 'string') {
861	      filterValue = value.toLowerCase();
862	    } else if (value?.title) {
863	      filterValue = value.title.toLowerCase();
864	    } else {
865	      return data; // If value is an unexpected type, return the full dataset
866	    }
867	
868	    return data.filter(item =>
869	      item?.RFQGroup_name
870	        ? item.RFQGroup_name.toLowerCase().includes(filterValue)
871	        : item?.title?.toLowerCase().includes(filterValue)
872	    );
873	  }

1009	  async onSaveItem(dateModified, saveAsDraft: boolean = true) {
1010	    let loaderIncremented = false;
1011	    try {
1012	      this.isSaving = true;
1013	      // Start loader when saving product
1014	      this.loaderService.increment();
1015	      loaderIncremented = true;
1016	
1017	      if (this.data.option != 1 && this.data.option != 2) {
1018	        if (this.requestQuoteMode) {
1019	          if (!this.priceCodeValue) {
1020	            this.priceCodeValue = await this.getUniqueRequestQuotePriceCode(this.data.requestQuoteSourceItem);
1021	          }
1022	        } else {
1023	          const priceCodeData: any = await firstValueFrom(this.itemService.generatePriceCode());
1024	          if (!this.STDImport) {
1025	            this.priceCode[4] = '-';
1026	            this.priceCode[5] = priceCodeData.generatedNumber;
1027	            this.priceCodeValue = this.priceCode.join('');
1028	          }
1029	        }
1030	
1031	        if (!this.validateRequiredFields()) {
1032	          this.showValidationError();
1033	          return;
1034	        }
1035	
1036	        const { dilution, logistics_load } = this.getCustomerDetails();
1037	        this.prepareNewItem(dateModified, dilution, logistics_load, this.requestQuoteMode ? saveAsDraft : true);
1038	
1039	        if (this.data.option == 0 || this.sourceLibraryItemId != null) {
1040	          this.addRoyality();
1041	          this.finalizeNewItem();
1042	
1043	          await firstValueFrom(this.itemService.addRfqItem({ data: this.newItem }));
1044	          this.dialogRef.close(this.newItem);
1045	        } else {
1046	          this.addRoyality();
1047	          this.finalizeNewItem();
1048	          this.handleSTDImport();
1049	
1050	          if (this.requestQuoteMode) {
1051	            await firstValueFrom(this.itemService.addRfqItem({
1052	              data: this.newItem,
1053	              STDImport: this.newItem.STDImport,
1054	              stdSelectedItem: this.newItem.stdSelectedItem,
1055	            }));
1056	          }
1057	          this.dialogRef.close(this.newItem);
1058	        }
1059	      }
1060	      else if (this.data.option == 1) {
1061	        if (!this.selectedValues.length) {
1062	          this._snackBar.open('Please select a licensor before saving.', 'OK', { duration: 4000 });
1063	          return;
1064	        }
1065	        await this.handleOption1();
1066	      }
1067	      else if (this.data.option == 2) {
1068	        if (!this.selectedValues.length) {
1069	          this._snackBar.open('Please select a licensor before saving.', 'OK', { duration: 4000 });
1070	          return;
1071	        }
1072	        await this.handleOption2();
1073	      }
1074	    } catch (error) {
1075	      this.handleError(error);
1076	    } finally {
1077	      this.isSaving = false;
1078	      // Decrement loader after save completes (dialog will close)
1079	      if (loaderIncremented) {
1080	        this.loaderService.decrement();
1081	      }
1082	    }
1083	  }

1147	  private hasValidAutocompleteSelection(control: UntypedFormControl, options: any[]): boolean {
1148	    const value = control.value;
1149	    if (value == null) {
1150	      return false;
1151	    }
1152	
1153	    if (typeof value === 'string') {
1154	      const trimmed = value.trim();
1155	      if (!trimmed) {
1156	        return false;
1157	      }
1158	      if (!options.length) {
1159	        return false;
1160	      }
1161	      return options.some((opt) => opt?.title?.toLowerCase() === trimmed.toLowerCase());
1162	    }
1163	
1164	    if (typeof value === 'object' && value.title?.trim()) {
1165	      if (!options.length) {
1166	        return true;
1167	      }
1168	      return options.some(
1169	        (opt) =>
1170	          (opt.id != null && value.id != null && opt.id === value.id) || opt.title === value.title,
1171	      );
1172	    }
1173	
1174	    return false;
1175	  }

## Investigation Report: RFQ Field Behavior Bugs

I have thoroughly investigated the three reported bugs in the DesignFlow Angular frontend. Here are my findings with exact file:line citations and root causes.

---

### BUG C: "Factory ID" rendered as plain number input instead of factory dropdown/autocomplete

**Confirmation: REAL BUG (confirmed)**

**Location:** 
- `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\sample_tracking\create-dialog\create-dialog.component.html` : lines 40-41
- `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\sample_tracking\create-dialog\create-dialog.component.ts` : lines 41, 50

**Current Implementation (Buggy):**
```html
<!-- Line 40-41 in create-dialog.component.html -->
<mat-form-field appearance="outline" subscriptSizing="dynamic" class="w-num">
  <mat-label>Factory ID</mat-label>
  <input matInput type="number" [(ngModel)]="model.factory_id_fk" />
</mat-form-field>
```

```typescript
// Line 50 in create-dialog.component.ts
factory_id_fk: null,
```

**Root Cause:**
The Factory ID field uses a plain `<input type="number">` which only accepts numeric input. The component has no dropdown/autocomplete configuration, no list of available factories, and no cellEditor assignment.

**Correct Pattern Exists:** YES
A working factory autocomplete/dropdown component exists at:
- `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\components\factory\factory.component.ts` : lines 73-260
  - Uses `MatAutocompleteModule` with a factory list (`factories: FactoryListRow[]` populated from `listFactories()`)
  - Implements a `displayFactoryFn` (line 93-101) that returns factory name from a `FactoryListRow` object
  - User selects by factory name, internally stores `f.id` (the numeric ID)
  - Provides a factory search/autocomplete pattern ready to reuse

The sample tracking module also has customer autocomplete (`C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\sample_tracking\create-dialog\create-dialog.component.ts` lines 31-32, 64-67) which demonstrates the same pattern.

**Why This Matters:**
Users cannot easily discover which factory they need. They must know the factory ID ahead of time, making data entry error-prone. The frontend should show human-readable factory names in a dropdown/autocomplete while storing the numeric ID.

---

### BUG D: "Retail price" does not format as currency/$ amount

**Confirmation: REAL BUG (confirmed)**

**Location:** 
- RFQ Grid: `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\helpers\ag-grid\rfq-grid\rfq.grid.config.ts` : line 112
- Sample Tracking Grid: `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\helpers\ag-grid\sample.tracking.config.ts` : lines 85-90 (fixed pattern)
- Create Sample Dialog: `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\sample_tracking\create-dialog\create-dialog.component.html` : lines 43-46 (buggy)

**RFQ Grid Current Implementation (Buggy):**
```typescript
// Line 112 in rfq.grid.config.ts — FOB Cost column, retail price NOT shown
addColumn("FOB Cost", "fob_cost", 130, { roles: ['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'production'] }),
```
No column definition exists for "Retail price" in the RFQ grid. When searching for `retail_price` or `comp_retail` (the RFQ field name), the RFQ grid column definition at line 110 is:
```typescript
// Line 110 in rfq.grid.config.ts
addColumn("Comp Retail", "comp_retail", 130, { roles: ['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'sales', 'designer'] }),
```
This column has **no `valueFormatter`** defined, so raw numbers display without currency formatting.

**Sample Tracking Create Dialog (Buggy):**
```html
<!-- Lines 43-46 in create-dialog.component.html -->
<mat-form-field appearance="outline" subscriptSizing="dynamic" class="w-num">
  <mat-label>Retail price</mat-label>
  <input matInput type="number" [(ngModel)]="model.retail_price" />
</mat-form-field>
```
Plain `<input type="number">` with no currency display.

**Correct Pattern Exists:** YES
Sample Tracking Grid at `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\helpers\ag-grid\sample.tracking.config.ts` : lines 85-95:
```typescript
addColumn('Retail', 'retail_price', 'retail_price', 110, {
  filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter',
  editable: true,
  type: 'numericColumn',
  valueFormatter: moneyFormatter  // <-- Currency formatter applied
}),
addColumn('FOB Cost', 'fob_cost', 'fob_cost', 110, {
  filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter',
  editable: true,
  type: 'numericColumn',
  valueFormatter: moneyFormatter  // <-- Currency formatter applied
}),
```

And the formatter is defined at lines 108-113:
```typescript
function moneyFormatter(params: any): string {
  const v = params && params.value;
  if (v === null || v === undefined || v === '') return '';
  const n = Number(v);
  return isNaN(n) ? String(v) : `$${n.toFixed(2)}`;
}
```

**Why This Matters:**
Raw numbers (e.g., "1500") are ambiguous — users don't know if it's $1,500 or $15.00. Currency formatting ($1,500.00) makes financial data clear and professional.

---

### BUG E: "FOB cost" field accepts alphabetic/non-numeric characters

**Confirmation: REAL BUG (confirmed)**

**Location:** 
- RFQ Grid: `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\helpers\ag-grid\rfq-grid\rfq.grid.config.ts` : line 112
- Add New Product Dialog (indirect): `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\rfq\addNewProduct\addNewProduct.component.ts` : line 1323

**RFQ Grid Current Implementation (Buggy):**
```typescript
// Line 112 in rfq.grid.config.ts
addColumn("FOB Cost", "fob_cost", 130, { roles: ['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'production'] }),
```
This column has **no `cellEditor` specified**, **no `valueParser`**, and **no input validation**. By default, AG Grid will render it with the default text editor, allowing any input (letters, symbols, etc.).

**Sample Tracking Grid (Buggy):**
```html
<!-- Lines 47-50 in create-dialog.component.html -->
<mat-form-field appearance="outline" subscriptSizing="dynamic" class="w-num">
  <mat-label>FOB cost</mat-label>
  <input matInput type="number" [(ngModel)]="model.fob_cost" />
</mat-form-field>
```
While `type="number"` prevents browser text input in modern browsers, AG Grid cells do NOT have this protection by default.

**Correct Pattern Exists:** YES
Sample Tracking Grid at `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\helpers\ag-grid\sample.tracking.config.ts` : lines 91-96:
```typescript
addColumn('FOB Cost', 'fob_cost', 'fob_cost', 110, {
  filter: 'agNumberColumnFilter',  // <-- Number filter
  editable: true,
  type: 'numericColumn',           // <-- AG Grid numeric column type
  valueFormatter: moneyFormatter
}),
```

The `type: 'numericColumn'` property in AG Grid automatically applies numeric-only cell editor and input validation. Additionally, a custom `valueParser` can be added for stricter control:
```typescript
valueParser: (params: any) => {
  const v = params.newValue;
  if (v === null || v === undefined || v === '') return null;
  const n = Number(v);
  return isNaN(n) ? undefined : n; // Reject non-numeric
}
```

**Why This Matters:**
Allowing non-numeric input in cost fields corrupts data and breaks downstream calculations (duty rates, landed cost, margins all depend on parsing FOB cost as a number). Invalid characters like "123abc" will fail silently or cause NaN propagation.
